content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 15, 1990

Peter A. Bagatelos  
Bagatelos & Fadem  
601 California Street, Suite 1801  
San Francisco, CA 94106

RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-90-510

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because you have not identified the person on whose behalf you are writing, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2/

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice. The situation described in your letter involves an association which has a recipient committee (Committee A). The primary purpose of the committee is to oppose a particular statewide ballot measure in California. Committee B is a separate committee whose purpose is to oppose the same statewide ballot measure. Committee A has made direct contributions to Committee B. In addition, the association has made non-monetary contributions to Committee B. The contributions made by the association and Committee A are controlled by the same persons. You asked for guidance as to the manner in which the association's contributions should be reported.

Committee B will report receipt of non-monetary contributions made on its behalf by the association on Schedule C of its Form

__________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c)(3).)

420. Because Committee A is sponsored by the association, the association's non-monetary contributions to Committee B may be disclosed on the allocation page of Committee A's Form 420 when no expenditure has been made from the association's general fund (e.g., the association has contributed employee services or goods already on hand). If the association makes expenditures out of its general fund on behalf of Committee B, Committee A will be required to itemize those expenditures as contributions from the association on its Schedule A and report the expenditures made on Schedule E as contributions to Committee B with a notation by each expenditure that it was made from the sponsor's general fund. In addition, Committee A must report the contributions on the allocation page. (Olson Advice Letter No. I-90-515, copy enclosed.)

If you have additional questions concerning this matter, please contact me at 916/322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant
